title: ophioglossales
intro: The ophioglossales rank is an order of vascular plants, characterized by having a unique reproductive structure called a sporophyll. This order includes various species of ferns and their allies, many of which are terrestrial and have creeping stems. The ophioglossales order includes several families, such as the Ophioglossaceae, which contains the genus Ophioglossum, and the various species within it, like Ophioglossum vulgatum. Other notable families in this order include the Selaginellaceae and the Psilotaceae, although the examples of families orchidaceae, euphorbiaceae and cyperaceae are not actually part of this order but belong to other ranks in the taxonomic hierarchy.

families.family_desc: Ophioglossaceae is a family of vascular plants commonly known as adder's tongue ferns.
